texto: And do you not think less of me for making my fortune insuch a way?” After all, her own sister did.He gave her an odd look. “There is no shame in being themistress of a king. It’s a position of great power and influence. I think less of Louis for letting you go.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Jenna Maclaine que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “And do you not think less of me for making my fortune insuch a way?” After all, her own sister di…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, love, self-judgement, com foco em self-judgement e the-righteous.

texto: Tonight I had wrought magic powerful enough to have drawn the attention of a goddess. In that moment, though, I was certain of one thing: Of all the magic I possessed, none of it could begin to compare to the magic he and I created together.
explicacao: Citação de Jenna Maclaine sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “Tonight I had wrought magic powerful enough to have drawn the attention of a goddess. In that mom…” articula amor, cin-craven, magic em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como boa-noite e reflexao-noturna.

frase_original: Tonight I had wrought magic powerful enough to have drawn the attention of a goddess. In that moment, though, I was certain of one thing: Of all the magic I possessed, none of it could begin to compare to the magic he and I created together.

texto: There is darkness inside all of us, though mine is more dangerous than most. Still, we all have it—that part of our soul that is irreparably damaged by the very trials and tribulations of life. We are what we are because of it, or perhaps in spite of it. Some useit as a shield to hide behind, others as an excuse to do unconscionable things. But, truly, the darkness is simply a piece of the whole, neither good nor evil unless you make it so. Ittook a witch, a war, and a voodoo queen to teach me that.
explicacao: Citação de Jenna Maclaine sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “There is darkness inside all of us, though mine is more dangerous than most. Still, we all have i…” articula reflexao, balance, cin-craven em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao-noturna e cin-craven.

frase_original: There is darkness inside all of us, though mine is more dangerous than most. Still, we all have it—that part of our soul that is irreparably damaged by the very trials and tribulations of life. We are what we are because of it, or perhaps in spite of it. Some useit as a shield to hide behind, others as an excuse to do unconscionable things. But, truly, the darkness is simply a piece of the whole, neither good nor evil unless you make it so. Ittook a witch, a war, and a voodoo queen to teach me that.
